attribution: Transcript data: TalkMoves dataset (SumnerLab / Suresh et al.), CC BY-NC-SA 4.0, speaker-blinded before any model call.
caveats: The calculator checks a claim only when it can ground it; 'not checked' means outside its current coverage, not wrong.
Reading a speaker's move is interpretive. The model proposes the reading; only the claim verdicts are computed.
One automated read of one transcript — a starting point for your own reading, not a verdict on the discussion.

# tm_0082

U0001 S01: I thought that it was kind of a neat way to do it.
U0002 S01: It was different from what  anybody else did.
U0003 S01: But, I still had a question.
U0004 S01: You sort of got at my question a  little bit, but I am not quite sure.
U0005 S01: Erik, you were going to say something?
U0006 S02: Well, see, if someone was to look at this for the first time, on the third number  line, for the first time, I know when I did I got confused because I thought that in  the middle of the section where she put the one third, two thirds and three thirds.
U0007 S02: I thought that, that, is where they would be.
U0008 S02: So that's why.
U0009 S02: I think that is what  Michael did, too.
U0010 S02: So, that is why he asked the question.
U0011 S01: How many of you thought that?
U0012 S01: How many of you did not think that?
U0013 S01: So, we all looked at it differently.
U0014 S01: Jessica?
U0015 S03: I think what Erik means is that he thought Meredith was making a whole new  number line; like, she thought two thirds was the half.
U0016 S02: Yeah.
U0017 S02: It looks confusing.
U0018 S01: But that was not what she was doing, was she?
U0019 S04: No.
U0020 S01: No.
U0021 S01: Alan what were you going to say?
U0022 S05: The way I thought, when you divide it up into fractions then you have a line  going to the fraction, but Meredith just put the fractions above the number line  without the line, so that meant she was labeling the area.
U0023 S01: Okay, now in the particular region over here, she has two thirds in this region, see  where I am pointing?
U0024 S04: Yes.
U0025 S01: Between the one third and the two thirds, in that region represent two thirds of the  line?
U0026 S02: It represent one third.
U0027 S01: OK, it represents one third; How do you know that?
U0028 S02: Because the way you said that one segment.
U0029 S02: Well, if you use  both those segments like the segment here to here, that would be two thirds, but  you said the segment here.
U0030 S02: It would be the two thirds the segment, but it would  be only one segment.
U0031 S01: Could you show me what represents two thirds, what part of that line represents  two thirds?
U0032 S02: Right to there.
U0033 S02: That would represent two thirds.
U0034 S01: What do you think?
U0035 S04: That is true.
U0036 S01: How many of you agree with that?
U0037 S05: Right, but what I think   Meredith was trying  to do was, you see how it had one third and the one third being here.
U0038 S05: She was  saying that she was labeling this to be the second third of   the line and labeling this to be the third, third of the line.
U0039 S05: Al.g
U0040 S01: Okay, so where would you put three over three, where would you write that  number if you were to write it on the number line?
U0041 S05: You are doing it the way Erik's talking about it, you would   put one third over that area and you would put two thirds in   that area and three thirds in that area, because this would be  representing one third,   both of those would be two thirds and   three of those would be three thirds.
U0042 S01: Ok, so if you were to put the number three thirds on the line?
U0043 S01: I see over here the  numbers zero,  one third, two thirds, and one..  Where would you put three thirds?
U0044 S05: If you put three thirds, you would put it just in that big area because it would be...
U0045 S02: No, you wouldn't.
U0046 S02: It would be right,  there.
U0047 S05: Right, that would be the mark of the three third,  but all three of those are the  three thirds.
U0048 S02: Meredith is saying right here that three thirds is right here, she says that is one,  three thirds in her perspective would be right here
U0049 S06: No.
U0050 S01: No.
U0051 S01: Let us hear what Meredith has to say.
U0052 S02: But …
U0053 S01: Let us hear what Meredith has to say.
U0054 S06: As I did over here, I am saying this area here is three thirds.
U0055 S02: I know; but, if someone were to look at this for the one time, they would think  that would be one whole and that would be three thirds, because you did not  label.
U0056 S02: You should label one whole and under it put three thirds.
U0057 S01: But, I did hear Meredith say that this piece is three thirds, did you mean that Meredith?
U0058 S06: Yeah, this whole entire piece right here
U0059 S01: You mean that?
U0060 S06: Yeah.
U0061 S02: And another thing Alan said …
U0062 S05: Well, that is why I said …
U0063 S02: I agree that would be the first piece.
U0064 S02: That is the second piece.
U0065 S02: But, together they make two-thirds.
U0066 S01: And what makes three thirds?
U0067 S05: Entire thing …
U0068 S02: Three thirds would be the entire thing, it would go right  here, if this is the third one third segment.
U0069 S05: Right, that is just representing the three-thirds.
U0070 S02: That's what I was talking about Alan, I am talking about, one section is one third,  one section is two thirds like what Meredith said, they are separate, when you put  them together they are two thirds.
U0071 S01: Ok.  Ok, let's hear from what other people are thinking.
U0072 S01: Brian?
U0073 S07: I know what Meredith is trying to say.
U0074 S07: She is trying to say—to label—between  it, because if someone said—if someone looks at it—and they did not know a  thing about a number line, they would probably think that the one third would be  between the one third and two thirds, that is why she labeled it also in the middle,  because if someone saw it and did not know what a number line was, they would  probably think one third is between the one third mark and the two third mark, so  she is just labeling it in the middle to make it less confusing.
U0075 S01: Ok, Amy and Jakki, you were going to say something?
U0076 S08: and  Jakki
U0077 S01: Not going to say any more?
U0078 S01: Ok.  Any one else have a comment?
U0079 S01: Ok, let me ask  you ….
U0080 S01: You have a comment, yes, Mark?
U0081 S09: I agree with Bryan because if no one ever saw a  number line, they would think that this was one third  and this was two thirds.
U0082 S01: Ok, mathematicians conventionally, what they do so that people are not confused  they kind of agree to a way to mark those numbers on the line, they have a  common way to agree.
U0083 S01: And, the common way to agree, you can see here that we  have a big number line, we take it apart in a minute, but you see where we put our  numbers, we place them where Meredith placed them.
U0084 S01: That is the way  Mathematicians do it, and if you want to know where those numbers go you  usually look on the bottom of the line, ok, and that sort of helps us understand the  why we have the notation.
U0085 S01: Alan?
U0086 S05: We could do that so that we could represent one half, two thirds, and   three thirds in pieces.
U0087 S05: Here is one way to  do it.
U0088 S05: Now, here would be one mark, two marks.
U0089 S05: Now, you could  could take out one of those pieces and say it is one third.
U0090 S01: What do you think, you like that?
U0091 S01: How many of you like that?
U0092 S01: How many had questions about?
U0093 S01: Okay.
U0094 S01: Thank you.
U0095 S01: Alan, stay there, let me ask you another question.
U0096 S01: On the first  number line you wrote up there, could you place the fractions zero,  one third,  two thirds, and three thirds?
U0097 S01: What about zero?
U0098 S05: Zero would be put here.
